attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; succeeds in mass sprints through late positional patience and timing in final kilometer. Showed early ability to take breakaway intermediate sprint opportunities as younger rider.
team_tactics: Historically deployed in support roles at Deceuninck/Quick-Step despite possessing sprint speed. First WorldTour wins came when given protected status rather than domestique duties.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finishes where positioning and timing matter more than pure power. Can animate breakaways for intermediate prizes but primary threat is in bunch kicks.
vulnerability: Years in Quick-Step support role suggest team may have underutilized sprint capabilities. Limited observations make it unclear how he performs when marked as primary threat versus opportunistic winner.
key_patterns: Late race positioning discipline: won from patient blue-and-white position in final kilometer
Opportunistic finisher: first WorldTour wins came when designated sprinters neutralized or mechanicked
Early career breakaway skills: demonstrated ability to contest intermediates from breaks as young pro
form_trajectory: positive: breakthrough WorldTour wins in 2020 after years in support roles suggest ascending trajectory with proper team backing
